why_this_action: The latest audit explicitly says to avoid another same-family directory or curator burst and to use the next active cycle on a different lane that can create clearer demand or cleaner follow-through measurement.
StackOverflow remains the highest-intent problem/solution lane already prepared in the repo and closest to real evaluator intent.

The lane was partially broken by an avoidable search-order and rate-limit-burn pattern, so repairing it in the same run improves the odds of future real placement instead of wasting more cycles on noisy reruns.
The preserved top target directly matches Ralph Workflow's strongest pain frame: making autonomous coding reviewable, verifiable, and safe enough for production-style work.

repair_summary: Reordered searches so the proven production-reliability query runs first.
Removed the bad-request-prone tagged workflow query.
Added stop-after-429 behavior to preserve the Stack Exchange quota window.
result.rate_limit_state: live Stack Exchange API still returned HTTP 429; previous best lane state was preserved intentionally

expected_outcome: A cleaner, less wasteful StackOverflow demand-capture lane plus a still-live answer packet ready for the next authenticated/manual posting opportunity.
replacement_condition: If the lane is still API-limited and unplaced by 2026-05-31, stop spending fresh cycles on StackOverflow discovery and move the next action slot to another executable high-intent lane instead of retrying the same search loop again.
